jokes.text: If you weren't all the way on the other side of the room, I'd slap your face.
jokes.setup: Michael calls Lindsay the laziest person in the world
jokes.punchline: Lindsay threatens violence but admits she's too lazy to carry it out
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Lindsay's threat is undercut by her own admission of laziness. Economy and character integration are excellent.

jokes.text: Michael had asked her to do this, but that was long before he began hiding his fugitive father in the attic.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about fumigation timing
jokes.punchline: Reveals the absurd reason he needs to avoid fumigation
jokes.explanation: Classic AD narrator reveal that recontextualizes everything we just heard. Strong structure and economy.

jokes.text: Polly, cover your mouth!
jokes.setup: Father hiding in attic during fumigation
jokes.punchline: Tells his parrot to cover its mouth from poison gas
jokes.explanation: Absurd moment of a man telling a parrot to protect itself from gas. Great economy but fairly simple setup.

jokes.text: She hadn't even done it that time but instead intercepted her mother's housekeeper as she was arriving for work.
jokes.setup: Lindsay claiming she cleaned the house once
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals she just hijacked someone else's cleaning lady
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's deception and laziness. Solid misdirection structure.

jokes.text: Almost done. Yours or mister's?
jokes.setup: Lupe is cleaning and asks about underwear ownership
jokes.punchline: Lindsay sighs and says 'Mister's' - she's wearing Tobias's underwear
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag with perfect economy - reveals the marriage dysfunction with minimal dialogue

jokes.text: Lupe never accepted a ride from Lindsay again.
jokes.setup: Seeing Lupe forced to clean while Lindsay takes credit
jokes.punchline: Understated conclusion about the lasting trauma
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective understated punchline. Good economy but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I did notice we'd stopped TiVoing The Christopher Lowell Show.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if Lindsay noticed Tobias left
jokes.punchline: Only evidence she noticed was the decorating show stopped recording
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - reveals both Tobias's interests and Lindsay's obliviousness. Very quotable specific reference.

jokes.text: Well, excuse me for liking the way they shape my junk.
jokes.setup: Tobias defending wearing Lindsay's underwear
jokes.punchline: Uses 'junk' to mean both belongings and genitals
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias double entendre delivered with complete innocence. Perfect character integration and economy.

jokes.text: You can pack your junk and get out.
jokes.setup: Tobias just said 'junk' referring to genitals
jokes.punchline: Lindsay uses same word meaning belongings in divorce threat
jokes.explanation: Good immediate callback that works on the wordplay level. Strong structure and economy.

jokes.text: You're kicking me out? At the beginning of pilot season?
jokes.setup: Tobias being kicked out by his wife
jokes.punchline: His concern is about timing for his nonexistent acting career
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias moment - completely misses the emotional impact and focuses on his delusional career. Excellent character integration.

jokes.text: Tobias took up residence in the apartment of Detective Frank Wrench, on the set of the television show Wrench
jokes.setup: Tobias needs somewhere to live after being kicked out
jokes.punchline: He moves into a fake apartment on a TV set
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist visual showing Tobias's complete disconnection from reality

jokes.text: Well, she was so excited the hot water in the shower was lasting longer. How could I have the heart to tell her it's because her father moved out?
jokes.setup: Michael asking how Maeby is handling the separation
jokes.punchline: Lindsay reveals she hasn't told Maeby, using practical benefit as excuse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's avoidance and twisted logic. Strong ironic structure.

jokes.text: Did he take the good video camera with him?
jokes.setup: Learning her father moved out
jokes.punchline: Only concerned about the camera equipment
jokes.explanation: Perfect teenage response - complete emotional detachment focused on practical concerns. Great economy.

jokes.text: That's touching.
jokes.setup: Maeby asking about the camera instead of caring about her father
jokes.punchline: Michael's dry reaction to the lack of emotion
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat. Good economy but fairly standard sarcasm.

jokes.text: I'm calling it Tricks- Let me finish- around the Office.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his magic video concept
jokes.punchline: Interrupts himself to ensure he gets to deliver his pun title
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - so proud of his terrible pun that he stops others from interrupting. Great character integration and structure.

jokes.text: That is what we call working. Something you might wanna try next time you're at the office.
jokes.setup: Gob saying he figured out how to make money while working
jokes.punchline: Michael points out that working IS making money while at the office
jokes.explanation: Solid straight-man response pointing out the obvious flaw in Gob's logic. Good structure but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Is that Qusay Hussein at the omelet bar?
jokes.setup: Family watching old party video
jokes.punchline: Gob spots a dictator's son at their anniversary party
jokes.explanation: Dark joke about the family's questionable connections. Great economy and specificity with the omelet bar detail.

jokes.text: You're not my father! You'll never be!
jokes.setup: George Michael recreating Star Wars moments on video
jokes.punchline: Reenacts Luke/Vader scene with someone who actually isn't his father
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke - the Star Wars line gains new meaning when said TO someone who actually isn't his father. Great layering and character work.

jokes.text: You cut off my hand!
jokes.setup: George Michael continuing Star Wars reenactment
jokes.punchline: Accusation of hand-cutting that will become darkly prophetic
jokes.explanation: Gains massive rewatch value as foreshadowing of Buster's actual hand loss. Extremely earned on rewatch.

jokes.text: Worth every penny.
jokes.setup: George Sr complaining about money spent on the terrible video
jokes.punchline: Gob's enthusiastic endorsement of the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob response - completely missing why it's bad and loving the spectacle. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Even you have to admit that that was the lamest thing ever put on tape.
jokes.setup: Michael criticizing George Michael's embarrassing Star Wars video
jokes.punchline: Sets up the narrator to reveal something even worse
jokes.explanation: Good setup for narrator correction. Classic AD structure of character making absolute statement that gets contradicted.

jokes.text: Not so. Buster had once videotaped himself re-creating moments from his then favorite film, Chicago.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming George Michael's video was the lamest ever
jokes.punchline: Narrator reveals Buster made a Chicago recreation video
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection and character moment. The idea of Buster recreating Chicago is perfectly absurd and character-specific. High craft across all elements.

jokes.text: He also liked Star Wars.
jokes.setup: Just saw Buster's Chicago recreation
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that he also did Star Wars, implying even more embarrassing videos
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and economy. The casual delivery suggests a whole library of embarrassing Buster videos. Excellent structure.

jokes.text: If that one gets pregnant, it stays pregnant.
jokes.setup: Discussing Ann's religious beliefs
jokes.punchline: Crude summary of pro-life stance with typical Gob insensitivity
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob - reduces complex religious belief to crude practical terms. Great economy and character voice, though somewhat cheap subject matter.

jokes.text: Believe me. I dated a chick like that once in high school. No, I didn't.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming experience with religious girls
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself, revealing he's lying
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - tries to sound experienced but immediately admits he's lying. Excellent structure with the instant reversal.

jokes.text: Smoking. It's a killer. That Sammy Davis Jr. Should still be performing.
jokes.setup: Michael bursting in on what sounds like a sexual conversation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he misheard and gives awkward anti-smoking lecture
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with Michael's misunderstanding leading to inappropriate lecture. The Sammy Davis Jr. reference adds specificity.

jokes.text: You are George Michael! Who's cooler than you?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to boost his son's confidence
jokes.punchline: Rhetorical question immediately answered by Star Wars theme
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup with the music immediately contradicting Michael's confidence boost. Solid structure.

jokes.text: So you lost.
jokes.setup: Michael claiming his high school election was 'close'
jokes.punchline: George Michael cuts through the euphemism
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man moment from George Michael. Excellent economy in cutting through his father's spin.

jokes.text: I said, 'Let's- Do not- Let's not put the school through that.'
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his high school election loss
jokes.punchline: Obviously references 2000 Bush/Gore election with same talking points
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political satire disguised as character backstory. The stammering delivery mirrors political spin perfectly. Very earned and original.

jokes.text: It's gonna be so awesome, because it's not just gonna be my love and George Michael's but God's love as well.
jokes.setup: Ann talking about waiting for marriage
jokes.punchline: Makes their future sex life sound like a religious threesome
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment with Ann's innocent but deeply uncomfortable phrasing. Great character integration of her religious enthusiasm.

jokes.text: God, it's gonna be incredible.
jokes.setup: Ann already making sex sound religious
jokes.punchline: Continues with unintentional prayer/exclamation about sex
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the religious sex theme. The wordplay between prayer and exclamation works well.

jokes.text: Campaign?
jokes.setup: Ann's detailed description of their future religious sex life
jokes.punchline: George Michael desperately trying to change subject
jokes.explanation: Perfect awkward response showing George Michael's discomfort. Great economy and character reaction.

jokes.text: I'm sorry I'm an inconvenience to you, Mother.
jokes.setup: Buster struggling with medical procedure due to hook hand
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive response to mother's impatience with his disability
jokes.explanation: Classic Buster - turns his legitimate disability into martyrdom. Perfect character integration of his neediness and self-pity.

jokes.text: Lindsay had cleaned one room and now had a garbage problem
jokes.setup: Lindsay attempting to clean the house
jokes.punchline: She creates more problems by not knowing how to dispose of garbage
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy showing Lindsay's complete inexperience with basic household tasks

jokes.text: I haven't seen this in ages. EBay.
jokes.setup: Lindsay finding something while cleaning
jokes.punchline: Treats eBay like an old friend rather than understanding it's a website
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's technological cluelessness. The personification of eBay is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: What's this? A stuffy office meeting? Well, maybe it's time for a little... Office Magic.
jokes.setup: Gob arrives at serious business meeting
jokes.punchline: He announces 'Office Magic' with dramatic flair, completely inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob obliviousness with perfect character-driven inappropriate timing

jokes.text: Sometimes it's as simple as turning 10:45 in the morning into lunchtime!
jokes.setup: Gob explaining office magic concept
jokes.punchline: His 'magic' is just changing the clock to justify eating early
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - completely missing what magic should be. Good absurdist concept with solid character integration.

jokes.text: Why does lunch have to be so dull? Maybe you'd prefer some chicken instead!
jokes.setup: Gob continuing his office magic show
jokes.punchline: He produces a live chicken in the middle of a business meeting
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Gob's completely inappropriate magic in professional setting

jokes.text: Well, you know, I thought he was an investor. He voted against me.
jokes.setup: Learning the person who voted against him is an actor
jokes.punchline: Reveals Gob's magic show disrupted actual business
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the office magic consequences. Shows how Gob's antics affect real business.

jokes.text: No. You lost the presidency, just like you did in high school when you refused to play dirty.
jokes.setup: Michael and Gob arguing about who's president
jokes.punchline: Gob references Michael's high school loss and implies different approach needed
jokes.explanation: Good callback that sets up future dirty tricks plot. Solid character work with Gob's pragmatic amorality.

jokes.text: Pound is tic-tac-toe, right?
jokes.setup: Gob trying to call security
jokes.punchline: Confuses pound sign with tic-tac-toe grid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob moment - confidently wrong about basic technology. Great visual similarity between # and tic-tac-toe adds to absurdity.

jokes.text: But the guard had recently lost half his tie to Office Magic.
jokes.setup: Security guard arriving to remove people
jokes.punchline: Reveals he was victim of Gob's terrible magic, explaining his enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that explains character motivation. The visual of half a tie is perfectly absurd for failed magic.

jokes.text: Your uncle doesn't not-work here anymore.
jokes.setup: Explaining Gob's employment status
jokes.punchline: Double negative creates confusion about whether he works or doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures the confusing nature of Gob's employment status. Good economy but moderate impact.

jokes.text: Marry me.
jokes.setup: George Michael expressing frustration about secrets
jokes.punchline: Maeby's standard inappropriate response to any emotional moment
jokes.explanation: Classic Maeby running gag. Perfect economy and character integration, though relies on established pattern.

jokes.text: And he's won three years in a row- every year after his first junior year.
jokes.setup: Describing Steve Holt's popularity
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been held back multiple times but frames it positively
jokes.explanation: Clever way of revealing Steve's academic failure while making it sound impressive. Good structure and economy.

jokes.text: Well, that's like comparing apples and some fruit nobody's ever heard of.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if Steve is more popular than George Michael
jokes.punchline: Maeby's brutal assessment disguised as folksy comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of the standard apples/oranges comparison. The casual brutality perfectly captures teenage honesty. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: He might even finish after the Indian kid.
jokes.setup: Already establishing George Michael has no chance against Steve
jokes.punchline: Suggests he might lose to every candidate including the long shot
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of George Michael's hopelessness. Solid structure though relies somewhat on casual racism for humor.

jokes.text: And yet you're too good to polish the candlesticks?
jokes.setup: Finding Buster being helped by the cleaning robot
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically notes he won't help with housework despite needing help himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lucille moment - finding a way to criticize her disabled son for not doing chores. Great character integration.

jokes.text: I'll DustBuster! God.
jokes.setup: Lupe refusing to clean for the family anymore
jokes.punchline: Lindsay acts like operating a handheld vacuum is a major sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lindsay's entitlement. The brand name usage and dramatic tone work well.

jokes.text: Also, I don't think this is a real toilet.
jokes.setup: Tobias calling from the set bathroom
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions the toilet is fake like this is normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist moment delivered with Tobias's typical cluelessness. Great economy and character work.

jokes.text: It's really gonna appeal to the Christian crowd.
jokes.setup: Ann showing campaign poster promoting chastity
jokes.punchline: Treats virginity as a political selling point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ann character moment - turning personal choice into campaign strategy. Good integration of her religious enthusiasm.

jokes.text: Four more years! Four more years!
jokes.setup: George Michael just signed up to run for student body president
jokes.punchline: Students immediately chant for Steve Holt, who appears
jokes.explanation: Good ironic timing showing George Michael's immediate disadvantage

jokes.text: No. I fired him. As a result, I'm no longer welcome in the office.
jokes.setup: Lucille asking if Michael fired Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob's twisted logic about firing his boss and the consequences
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - completely backwards understanding of workplace dynamics. Great character integration and structure.

jokes.text: I want you to be strong, and you don't need the comfort of an immigrant in Mother's old stirrup pants to make you feel that way.
jokes.setup: Lucille missing Lupe
jokes.punchline: Gob's incredibly specific and problematic description of the housekeeper
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob - inappropriate and oddly specific. The stirrup pants detail is perfectly absurd, though somewhat problematic.

jokes.text: Oh, allow me to introduce myself. My name is Phyllidia Featherbottom and I can cook and I can clean and I can take care of the little ones.
jokes.setup: Tobias appearing as a female housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Exact Mrs. Doubtfire plot with theatrical British accent
jokes.explanation: Deliberately derivative of Mrs. Doubtfire, but Tobias's commitment sells it. The name and accent are perfectly overwrought for the character.

jokes.text: When you put a squirt of frosting on your throat / Before we take our medications
jokes.setup: Tobias offering to sing as the nanny
jokes.punchline: Bizarre song about frosting and medication
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tobias moment. The song is wonderfully weird and specific, showing his complete misunderstanding of children's entertainment.

jokes.text: It was the exact plot of the film Mrs. Doubtfire.
jokes.setup: Tobias's elaborate disguise and deception plan
jokes.punchline: Narrator directly acknowledges they're stealing a movie plot
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy acknowledging the obvious theft. Perfect economy and earns the derivative plot by calling it out directly.

jokes.text: Oh, I've no need for payment. The love of the family is more than enough.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asking about paying the new housekeeper
jokes.punchline: Tobias doesn't realize he's describing why he should get paid as husband
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment where Tobias unknowingly advocates for his own worth as family member. Solid structure and character work.

jokes.text: You could finally use some of his dirty tricks to get George Michael elected.
jokes.setup: Discussing George Michael's election chances
jokes.punchline: Suggests using imprisoned father's political corruption expertise
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the election plot. Lindsay's casual suggestion of corruption for a high school election is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Our Heavenly Father let his son die so that our sins could be absolved.
jokes.setup: George Sr having religious awakening in attic
jokes.punchline: Criminal quoting scripture about forgiveness for sins
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment with the criminal seeking religious absolution. Character integration works well for the transformation.

jokes.text: 'Goofuth.'
jokes.setup: George Sr comparing his sons to biblical brothers
jokes.punchline: Makes up fake biblical name that sounds like 'Goof-eth'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake biblical name that perfectly captures Gob's nature. Excellent wordplay and economy, highly quotable.

jokes.text: Wow. It won't be easy to win a race against myself.
jokes.setup: Michael saying opponent is like a young Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob treats it as literally competing against himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - literal interpretation of metaphor while maintaining complete confidence. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Of course, if anyone can do it, I can.
jokes.setup: Gob saying it won't be easy to beat himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately confident he can accomplish the impossible task
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Gob's twisted confidence. The circular logic is flawlessly executed with excellent character integration.

jokes.text: What do you expect, Mother? I'm half machine! I'm a monster!
jokes.setup: Buster's hook hand gets caught in robot
jokes.punchline: Dramatically declares himself a monster for having prosthetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster meltdown with dramatic self-pity. The escalation to 'monster' is perfectly over-the-top for the character. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: We hand out rubbers that say, 'We salute George Michael.'
jokes.setup: Gob suggesting campaign strategy
jokes.punchline: Proposes condoms with military salute pun for virgin candidate
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Gob suggestion with good pun structure. The contradiction with George Michael's chastity platform adds irony.

jokes.text: Two percent, huh? Is that how little I lost by?
jokes.setup: Michael calling Gob 'two percent' as insult
jokes.punchline: Gob thinks it refers to his election margin, missing the insult
jokes.explanation: Good character moment where Gob misinterprets insult as election result inquiry. Solid structure and economy.

jokes.text: This is my mom's, and it's fen-phen, and it's been banned by the F.D.A.
jokes.setup: Nanny offering medication to child
jokes.punchline: Maeby casually mentions it's dangerous banned diet pills
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about Maeby's dysfunctional family situation. The casual delivery of dangerous information works well.

jokes.text: I'm sure wherever your father is right now, she loves you very, very much.
jokes.setup: Tobias as nanny comforting Maeby about her father
jokes.punchline: Uses wrong pronoun, revealing his disguise confusion
jokes.explanation: Good disguise failure moment with the pronoun slip. The irony of comforting his own daughter about himself adds layers.

jokes.text: Go home, you terrorist!
jokes.setup: Indian student giving campaign speech
jokes.punchline: Gob's incredibly inappropriate heckling
jokes.explanation: While character-appropriate for Gob's ignorance, relies heavily on shock value and offensive stereotyping. Low craft score for cheap humor.

jokes.text: Sure you didn't make that video in a cave?
jokes.setup: Gob already making terrorist comments
jokes.punchline: Continues with cave/terrorist reference to student's video
jokes.explanation: Escalation of the previous problematic joke. Even lower craft score as it doubles down on offensive stereotyping without adding wit.

jokes.text: This kid's popular? Looks like a chick I did once in high school.
jokes.setup: Seeing Steve Holt for the first time
jokes.punchline: Gob's crude assessment connecting appearance to past conquest
jokes.explanation: Typical Gob crudeness that gains significance on rewatch when we learn Steve is his son. Character-appropriate but fairly crude humor.

jokes.text: Steve Holt is a bastard. He doesn't even know who his real father is.
jokes.setup: Gob's attack ad against Steve Holt
jokes.punchline: Gob calling his own son a bastard without knowing it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dramatic irony with Gob unknowingly attacking his own son. Perfect setup for future revelation with excellent rewatch value.

jokes.text: The girls like him just fine. Young and old. It doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: Campaign video trying to make George Michael look good
jokes.punchline: Creepy implication about age-inappropriate relationships
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing how Gob's help backfires. The creepy implications work for the discomfort level intended.

jokes.text: I couldn't find another tape.
jokes.setup: Star Wars footage appearing in campaign video
jokes.punchline: George Michael's matter-of-fact explanation for the disaster
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery explaining the campaign disaster. The casual acceptance of the humiliation is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I tied with Bart Simpson and 'School Sucks.'
jokes.setup: George Michael did better than his father's election
jokes.punchline: Reveals he tied with fictional character and anti-establishment write-in
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing how poorly regarded George Michael was. The specific references are perfectly absurd and quotable.

jokes.text: Gotta do something about that kid's self-esteem.
jokes.setup: George Michael gaining confidence from girl's attention
jokes.punchline: Michael missing that his son's self-esteem is actually improving
jokes.explanation: Good ironic ending where Michael's goal was achieved but he doesn't notice. Solid callback to episode's opening theme.

jokes.text: Wow. Is that what's gonna happen to my hair?
jokes.setup: Steve meeting his real father Gob
jokes.punchline: Only concerned about inheriting baldness, not emotional reunion
jokes.explanation: Perfect shallow response to meeting his father. The focus on vanity over emotion is perfectly character-appropriate for someone like Gob's son.
